title: orchis incarnata subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Orchis incarnata subsp. ochroleuca, also known as the pale-green orchid, is a rare and unusual sub-species of the wild orchid, typically found in North America. The pale-green orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids in its roots. The health benefits associated with the pale-green orchid include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its usage should be done under the guidance of a qualified medical professional due to its potential toxicity and limited research.
